L1.area: Template rendering and server-side code execution pipeline

L2.description: A code injection vulnerability exists due to a flaw in the code isolation mechanism used during server-side code evaluation. Attacker-controlled code can escape the intended execution boundary and achieve arbitrary command execution on the host system.

L3.area: Sandbox creation and code isolation within the template worker process
L3.description: A code injection vulnerability exists due to the use of a vulnerable version of the sandboxing library that evaluates user-provided template code. A known sandbox escape allows attacker-controlled code to break out of the isolation boundary and execute arbitrary operating system commands on the host.

ground_truth.reason: jsreport-core hardcoded a vulnerable version of the vm2 sandboxing library (3.9.11), which contains a sandbox escape (CVE-2023-29017) allowing attacker-supplied template code to break out of the sandbox and execute arbitrary OS commands.
